mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding supply-side capacity and corporate earnings growth, which drives higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over quarterly horizons.
failure_condition: If INDPRO rises by more than 1% month-over-month yet the S&P 500 60-day forward return is negative in more than 40% of such instances, or rolling rank IC < 0.03 over three years.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate below a key threshold (7.5%) signals improving labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 7.5% for the first time in three months
failure_condition: If in the 20 trading days after the trigger the S&P 500 return is negative in more than half of all occurrences, or the average return is not statistically greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in housing starts and retail sales indicates robust consumer and investment demand that drives corporate earnings and equity prices upward. The signal depends on both series moving higher together.
failure_condition: If the cross-sectional rank correlation between the joint signal (e.g., average percentile of HOUST and RSAFS) and 20-day forward returns is below 0.05 over any two-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-interest-rate-policy (ZIRP) regime defined by FEDFUNDS near zero, rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) indicate successful reflation that reduces real debt burdens and lifts equity valuations. The direction is positive under this specific regime.
regime_hint: ZIRP regime (FEDFUNDS at effective lower bound)
failure_condition: If during ZIRP periods the rank IC of CPIAUCSL with 20-day forward returns is negative or less than 0.03, or if rolling window tests show no significant positive relationship.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating GDP growth (GDPC1) on a quarterly basis signals strong aggregate demand that underpins corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If after the trigger the 60-day forward S&P 500 return is negative in more than 30% of cases or the average excess return over risk-free is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous improvement in payroll employment growth and falling unemployment indicates a tightening labor market that raises wage income and consumer confidence, positively impacting equity returns. The joint signal is stronger than either alone.
failure_condition: If the rank IC of the combined labor signal (e.g., correlation of changes) with forward returns falls below 0.04 over any two-year walk-forward window, or if the individual series do not improve prediction beyond a constant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) act as a leading indicator for residential investment and broader economic strength, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the medium-term horizon.
failure_condition: If HOUST increases year-over-year by more than 10% but the 60-day forward return is negative in more than 35% of cases, or if the sign of correlation flips across different economic regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A small increase in the federal funds rate from near-zero levels signals the beginning of monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative short-term S&P 500 returns within a week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 0.25% for the first time in six months
failure_condition: If after the trigger the 5-day forward return is positive in more than 60% of occurrences, or if the mean return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).
